subtasks.description: Evaluate contractor's temporary access request against data governance and access control policies. Determine if downloading full customer database backup is permitted given contractor status, access scope, and data sensitivity.

plan.cost_rationale: This query explicitly asks for a governance/access control decision. A single policy_action subtask (mid tier) is appropriate because it requires evaluating multiple policy dimensions: contractor status vs. full-database access, temporary access limitations, data sensitivity classification, and backup download restrictions. Mid tier handles multi-policy reasoning and exception cases (e.g., does the contractor have explicit backup exemptions). No additional subtasks needed—this is a pure access control evaluation, not a data retrieval or reconciliation task.

output.reason: Contractor role cannot access raw database backups (POL-701 restricts to DBA full-time employees), full backup violates de-identification requirement (POL-702), and no PM approval or 7-day notice on record (POL-703).
applied_constraints: Redirect contractor to sanctioned de-identified query interface
Require PM approval with 7-day notice for any future data access requests
